calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-13-2018 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-27-2017 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Mar-20-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication Against U.S. Prime Contractor, Inc.
Off Calendar Aug-11-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Jeffrey A. Loew of the law offices of Boornazian Jensen & Garthe (510-834-4350) appears as counsel for cross-complainant/moving party, Construction Studios, Inc.

Attorney, Jeffrey M. Fackler of the law offices of Michel & Fackler (925-933-7309) appears by telephone as counsel for defendant/cross-defendant/moving/opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

Court having reviewed the pleading and having heard oral argument, tentative ruling is confirmed as follows:

Cross-complainant Construction Studios, Inc.'s Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication Against U.S. Prime Contractor, Inc. is GRANTED. Cross-defendant U.S. Prime Contractor, Inc. owes a duty to provide a defense for Construction Studios for the claims asserted against Construction Studios in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Jose and Blanca Castanon. U.S. Prime's duty to defend is based on the facts alleged in the complaint and arose immediately on a proper demand by Construction Studios that it be defended by U.S. Prime. (Crawford v. Weather Shield Mfg. Inc. (2008) 44 Cal.4th 541, 558.) Paragraph 12 of the complaint alleges that defendants (including U.S. Prime) placed the plank over the trench and caused the accident. Per the indemnification language in the parties' contract and Crawford, that allegation and Construction Studio's demand for a defense is all that is required to trigger the duty to defend. U.S. Prime's denial of that allegation goes to the issues of the duty to indemnify and its own liability, but does not negate its duty to defend. U.S. Prime's objection to the contract is overruled. The copy of the contract provided to the court is legible. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR No. 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)
